user_stories.goal: Enter destination, trip length, budget level, and interests to generate a personalised day-by-day itinerary
user_stories.benefit: Quickly receive a tailored travel plan that matches my constraints and interests
acceptance_criteria: A form accepts destination, start date or flexible start, trip length (in days), budget level (low/medium/high) and selectable interests (e.g., museums, food, outdoors)
Inputs are validated and user-relevant error messages are shown for missing/invalid values
Submitting the form calls the backend and returns a clear day-by-day itinerary in under 10 seconds for normal requests
Itinerary includes for each day: ordered activities, approximate times/durations, estimated costs per activity, and travel time between activities

user_stories.goal: View and interact with the generated itinerary on the website in a readable, printable format
user_stories.benefit: Easily follow, share, and print my travel plan while on trip
acceptance_criteria: Website displays itinerary as a clear day-by-day list with collapsible day sections and activity details
Each activity shows name, description, estimated duration, cost, and a link to a map or directions
User can print or export the itinerary to PDF and share a permalink to the itinerary
Layout is responsive and usable on mobile and desktop screens

user_stories.goal: Save and load my travel preferences and previous itineraries
user_stories.benefit: Avoid re-entering common preferences and reuse successful itineraries
acceptance_criteria: User can save preferences (budget, interests, preferred pace) to their account or local storage
Saved preferences pre-populate the form when the user returns
User can view a list of previously generated itineraries and load or duplicate any itinerary
Saved itineraries retain full details and are downloadable as PDF

user_stories.goal: Use a Flask API endpoint to programmatically generate itineraries from provided input JSON
user_stories.benefit: Allow other clients or services to integrate itinerary generation into their workflows
acceptance_criteria: Flask API exposes a documented POST /api/v1/generate endpoint that accepts destination, trip_length, budget, interests and optional start date
Endpoint returns a JSON itinerary with status 200 on success and appropriate error codes (400/422/500) for invalid requests
Responses include structured day objects, activity lists, estimated costs and metadata (generation timestamp, input echo)
API enforces basic rate limiting and returns meaningful error messages for malformed requests

user_stories.goal: Manage attractions, opening hours, and cost estimates so itineraries are accurate and up to date
user_stories.benefit: Improve itinerary quality and reduce user friction caused by outdated information
acceptance_criteria: Admin interface allows create/read/update/delete (CRUD) of attractions, categories, hours, and typical cost ranges
Changes made in the admin UI are reflected in new itinerary generations within a defined sync window (e.g., immediately or within 5 minutes)
Basic validation prevents saving attractions with incomplete mandatory data (name, location, category)
Audit log records who changed what and when for traceability
